channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump appoints Sara Carter as next drug czar, says she 'will lead the charge to protect' US
item.description: President Donald Trump appointed Sara Carter as drug czar, saying she will "lead the charge to protect our nation and save our children from the scourge of drugs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's election order creates much confusion before the next federal election in 2026
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to change how U.S. elections are run is creating uncertainty for state and local election officials and worries about voter confusion before the next federal election, the 2026 midterms.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump-blocking judges managed to get past Senate judiciary hawks
item.description: There is some disagreement among experts and Republicans over whether enough was done to block Democratic-nominated judges, particularly those who are now blocking the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here’s what happened during Trump’s 10th week in office
item.description: The Trump administration fielded questions after the Atlantic published a story detailing how administration officials used a Signal group chat to discuss strikes in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Utah bans LGBTQ+ pride flags, MAGA flags, other unapproved flags in government buildings, schools
item.description: Utah became the first state to prohibit LGBTQ+ pride flags from being displayed at government buildings and schools after the governor declined to reject the measure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hegseth’s younger brother is serving in a key role as liaison and senior adviser inside the Pentagon
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s younger brother is serving in a key position inside the Pentagon as a Department of Homeland Security liaison and senior adviser, Hegseth's office confirmed.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on acquiring Greenland for ‘world peace’ amid Vance visit
item.description: President Donald Trump has repeatedly offered, dating back to his first administration, a desire to secure Greenland for the U.S. as Russian and Chinese presence grows in the Arctic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Elon Musk to hand out $1M checks ahead of judicial race in Wisconsin
item.description: Elon Musk said he would be traveling to Wisconsin before the Supreme Court race to speak with voters and give out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump endorses budget fix that would restore Washington, DC, shortfall and urges House to pass it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is pushing the House of Representatives to pass a measure that would prevent immediate financial disaster for the District of Columbia even as he continues to level harsh criticism at the city and its leaders.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell: US headed for ‘Russia wins, America loses’ headline on Ukraine deal
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) warned in a speech Thursday that U.S. negotiators trying to end the war in Ukraine are headed for a “Russia wins, America loses” headline if they agree to a deal that achieves only “illusory peace.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Kentucky Gov. Andy Beshear displays his colors in veto fights with Republican lawmakers
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Knowing the odds of winning were stacked against him, Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear nevertheless vetoed bills sent his way by the Republican-dominated Kentucky legislature with by-now familiar themes: abortion, Medicaid and diversity initiatives.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noted economist honored by Trump warns his 25% tariffs could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Noted economist Arthur Laffer warns in a new analysis that President Donald Trump's 25% tariffs on auto imports could add $4,711 to the cost of a vehicle and says the proposed taxes could weaken the ability of U.S. automakers to compete with their foreign counterparts.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Republican chair challenges Trump over emergency funding move
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine), the head of the powerful Senate Appropriations Committee, and her Democratic counterpart are challenging the Trump administration’s handling of emergency funding included in legislation passed earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump: House should take up Senate-passed DC funding fix immediately
item.description: President Trump is urging swift passage of legislation to fix what members have called a “mistake” by Congress that forced significant cuts to Washington, D.C.’s local budget. Trump on Friday wrote on Truth Social that the House should “immediately” take up the “D.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DFLers at Minnesota Capitol rip Musk’s ideas while the GOP wants a closer look
item.description: Billionaire and top Trump adviser Elon Musk is reshaping the federal government. In Minnesota, Democrats are increasingly turning Musk mentions into a punching bag. And Republicans say his work could be replicated in St. Paul.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: GOP leaders accused of making threats to block bill to let new moms vote remotely
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties teamed up to force a House vote on a measure allowing new parents to vote by proxy for 12 weeks, but House Speaker Mike Johnson opposes it on Constitutional grounds.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Amid support from doctors group, bill to clarify Texas’ abortion ban does little to save lives, critics say
item.description: Senate Bill 31 supporters say it would clarify when doctors should intervene to save a pregnant woman’s life, but critics say its vagueness and a measure to resurrect pre-Roe laws hamper it.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Pooja Salhotra And Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: European institutions target 'scientific asylum-seekers' from US
item.description: The move comes as federal funds for research are cut and studies are subject to ideological limitations.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Congress questions the FAA, US Army and NTSB over deadly midair collision in DC
item.description: The head of the National Transportation Safety Board appeared alongside officials from the Federal Aviation Administration and the Army's aviation division at a hearing on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Fox 5

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about Minnesota’s government employee return to office plan
item.description: Gov. Tim Walz has directed workers to report to offices or job sites at least 50 percent of the time beginning in June. Employee unions are pushing back.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Clay Masters, and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: 5 takeaways from House hearing on NPR, PBS
item.description: The heads of the nation's top public broadcasters faced an intense grilling from lawmakers Wednesday over their outlets’ funding and editorial objectivity.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration says it will pull back billions in COVID funding from local health departments
item.description: Federal health officials said Tuesday they are pulling back $11.4 billion in COVID-19-related funds for local public health departments and other health organizations throughout the nation. Minnesota stands to lose $226 million.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and Erica Zurek, MPR News

item.title: DOGE says it needs to know the government’s most sensitive data, but can’t say why
item.description: DOGE staffers have skirted privacy laws, training and security protocols to gain virtually unfettered access to financial and personal information stored in siloed government databases.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler and Jenna McLaughlin, MPR News

item.title: Florida Democrats continue to push for Medicaid expansion
item.description: Amid fears of federal cuts, the battle continues at the statehouse as Florida Democrats continue to push the GOP super majority to consider expanding Medicaid.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
